name: Embalse de Valmayor – circular por trialeras desde Las Matas y Torrelodones
region_intro: Mountain biking around Las Rozas De Madrid offers access to diverse landscapes, bordering the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and the Regional Park of the Middle Course of the Guadarrama River. The terrain features pine and holm oak forests, green valleys, and granite formations, providing varied riding experiences. Within Las Rozas, the Dehesa de Navalcarbón offers extensive paths through pine forests, while the Guadarrama River provides picturesque riparian trails. The region's topography includes both gentle paths and more challenging climbs, with peaks exceeding 2000 meters in the nearby Sierra. Best mountain bike trails around Las Rozas De Madrid The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse Molino de la Hoz – circular desde Las Rozas de Madrid por El Gasco y senda del Rio Guadarrama, a 23.5 miles (37.8 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route follows the Guadarrama River and offers views of the Molino de la Hoz reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de Valmayor – circular por trialeras desde Las Matas y Torrelodones, a difficult 34.6 miles (55.8 km) path. This route explores the area around the Valmayor reservoir, featuring varied terrain and technical sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Batán Bridge – GR-124 Royal Trail loop from Las Matas, a 38.4 miles (61.8 km) trail leading through forests and along historic paths, often completed in about 5 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Las Rozas De Madrid is defined by pine forests, riparian trails along the Guadarrama River, and granite formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family routes to demanding trails. The routes in Las Rozas De Madrid are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Las Rozas De Madrid's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails around Las Rozas De Madrid, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. This includes 13 easy routes, 38 moderate routes, and 31 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: The terrain around Las Rozas De Madrid is quite diverse. You'll find trails through pine and holm oak forests, along the picturesque Guadarrama River, and near granite formations. The region borders the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and the Regional Park of the Middle Course of the Guadarrama River, offering varied landscapes from gentle paths to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: Yes, Las Rozas De Madrid offers several easy mountain bike routes suitable for families and beginners. Many paths within the Dehesa de Navalcarbón are ideal for a relaxed ride, and the broader network includes 13 easy tours designed for less experienced riders.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking?
A: You can explore various natural features and landmarks. The trails often follow the Guadarrama River, offering scenic views. You might encounter reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1456505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de la Hoz Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmot Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders, there are 31 difficult routes available. One challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959558525/embalse-de-valmayor-circular-por-trialeras-desde-las-matas-y-torrelodones" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Valmayor – circular por trialeras desde Las Matas y Torrelodones&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.8 km path with varied terrain and technical sections. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6939535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge – GR-124 Royal Trail loop from Las Matas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 61.8 km through forests and historic paths.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964245912/embalse-molino-de-la-hoz-circular-desde-las-rozas-de-madrid-por-el-gasco-y-senda-del-rio-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Molino de la Hoz – circular desde Las Rozas de Madrid por El Gasco y senda del Rio Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail that follows the Guadarrama River.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: The mountain bike trails in Las Rozas De Madrid are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 230 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find routes that include natural features like waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall – La Berzosa Viewpoint loop from Las Matas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47 km route that takes you past the Covacho Waterfall.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Las Rozas De Madrid?
A: Las Rozas de Madrid offers good mountain biking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While winter can bring snow to the higher elevations of the nearby Sierra de Guadarrama, many lower-lying trails remain accessible, though conditions can vary.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Dehesa de Navalcarbón?
A: Yes, the Dehesa de Navalcarbón, a large pine forest within Las Rozas, features numerous paths perfect for cycling. While specific routes are not listed here, you'll find many options within this area, offering a mix of forest trails suitable for various activities.

Q: Can I find routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Many trails in the region provide excellent viewpoints, especially those bordering the Sierra de Guadarrama. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall – La Berzosa Viewpoint loop from Las Matas&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes the La Berzosa Viewpoint, offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight you might encounter.

Q: Is there a route that connects to the Molino de la Hoz Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5318591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retamar Bridge – Molino de la Hoz Reservoir loop from Ciudad del Fútbol de Las Rozas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 39.1 km route that takes you around the Molino de la Hoz Reservoir, starting from Ciudad del Fútbol de Las Rozas.
tips:
It is not a single path, but 3. Each one has its grace, but all are a jewel to roll fast with a rigid MTB:

The first one goes along the channel of the canal, it is very smooth and fast, but beware that there are sections that flood easily or have a lot of grass.
the second one goes to the right of the riverbed (direction from Gasco to Las Matas), on top of the same terrace very close, it winds through the vegetation
and the third one goes to the left of the channel, somewhat higher and separates more from the channel, winds through more closed vegetation and will leave your arms very scratched, apart from hooking very often with the handlebars (and mine is not very wide ). It does not go as far as Las Matas, but connects with a couple of climbs that lead to the Gurugu. Recently they have put up signs that prohibit the passage through hunting grounds.

The section that reaches the viewpoint of the El Gasco dam is a pass, both up and down.
Personally, I prefer to upload it, as it allows you to enjoy the views much more. On the left hand side we will pass an embankment and, more or less towards the middle of the section, we will see the El Gasco dam appear in the distance.
The last section of the climb is quite technical, as it narrows a lot. In addition, large loose stones will put our skills to the test.
More at https://bigjoe.es/ruta-mtb-boadilla-presa-gasco/
Nice views of the La Hoz Reservoir, from the dam, and from the bridge that crosses it from the La Hoz Urbanization.
Part of the way was through a private farm. They have passed the plow with a tractor and destroyed it. I have asked Komoot to delete it
Mixed path with land areas and broken areas, with a couple of very steep ramps

It is the object of great devotion on the part of the beehives. It is located in a beautiful location on the road to the Valmayor swamp. Built in 1999, it is a simple construction, with beautiful windows, made of granite and slate roof. It has a viewpoint and on a rock there is a cruise that announces Mariana devotion with the stone image of Santiago Apostle.

In the esplanade there are two sources of drinking water where we can refill the drum
Crossing the dam bridge you can see wonderful views of the Sierra de Madrid, Los Siete Picos and La Bola del Mundo. You can also see the extension of the Valmayor Reservoir.

The El Gasco dam, also called Peña Strait or Carlos III Dam, is one of the most important civil engineering projects in 18th century Spain. It is located above the Guadarrama River gorge, in an area currently protected by the regional park of the middle course of the Guadarrama River and its surroundings.
Fun single track, technically very simple and with a lot of flow

Icon of the city of Torrelodones and appreciated by many when passing through the A-6 motorway. The current tower is a restoration, while very little remains of the original. The construction dates back to the 10th century when the area was under the command of the Caliphate of Cordoba and after being abandoned for centuries, it was rebuilt in 1930.
